text.title: Champion Challenger Pattern for RAG Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Failover from champion to challenger fires on production error; users see challenger output. Bench is contaminated.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated challenger as a hot spare; missed the no-leak rule.
antipattern.fix: Failover MUST be to previous champion's cache, never to challenger. Challenger response is observability-only.

antipattern.symptom: Challenger looks great at day 3; PM pushes promote-now. Day 7 numbers show variance; promotion was premature.
antipattern.fix: Fixed 7-day cycle; no mid-cycle promotion. Document the rule and refuse exceptions.

antipattern.symptom: Challenger wins by 2pp with n=150. After full rollout, real delta is -1pp.
antipattern.fix: Require n ≥ 1000 AND delta ≥ 3pp. Below either, bench another week.

antipattern.symptom: 6 months of bench cycles; ops can't reconstruct why version 4 was promoted over version 3.
antipattern.root-cause: Bench artefacts kept ad-hoc in shared docs.
antipattern.fix: Append-only audit log per decision: champion / challenger / judge / delta / n / decider / timestamp.
